Section 1. Identity, Background, and Public Status
1.1 Registry and Designations
The asset designated SLATEFALL is filed under the Pan-American Metahuman Cooperative (PAMC)
Master Registry as PAMC-A2-014, registered 19 March 2019. Operational classification: Class A-2
(Kinetic, Targeted). The codename was assigned by the PAMC Naming Subcommittee on 7 May 2019,
derived from the asset's powers-emergence event on the eastern slate face of Cerro Castillo, Aysén
Region, Chile.
Three additional designations remain in active rotation:
"Calva" — informal in-cell handle used within Cell Halcón
"0-3-Castillo" (zero-three-Castillo) — radio call sign for cross-cell coordination
"La Detención" — adversary-side designation observed in three intercepted Concession-
syndicate communications (2024); unofficial, not used internally
Her registry threat-to-handler rating is 0.7 / 5, the lowest among PAMC Class A-2 operatives currently
active.
1.2 Civilian Identity
The asset's civilian identity is Capt. (ret.) Inez Yolanda Calvache-Renström, born 8 September 1987 in
Punta Arenas, Magallanes Region, Chile. Father: Coronel (ret.) Hernán Calvache-Mardones, Chilean Army
military geologist (deceased 2017). Mother: Dr. Claire Renström-Verdugo, Franco-Chilean pediatrician
(retired 2019; resides Viña del Mar).

The family relocated to Santiago in late 1999 following her father's posting to the Army's Centro de
Estudios Geográficos. Calvache-Renström retained dual Chilean–French citizenship from birth; the
French citizenship is currently administratively suspended pending PAMC liaison clearance, last reviewed
Q3 2025.

2.content:
Section 2. Powers, Abilities, and Documented Limits
2.1 Core Mechanic: Inertial Suspension
The asset's primary power, designated Inertial Suspension (IS) under PAMC scientific cataloguing,
permits the targeted nullification of inertia in a single physical object within line-of-sight range. The effect
was first observed during the powers-emergence event of 8 October 2018 (see §3.4) and has been
continuously studied by the Cooperative Bureau of Metahuman Physics (CBMP) since March 2019.
The operative theory of mechanism — the Calvache–Marrón Equations — was developed jointly by the
asset and CBMP physicist Dr. Léon Marrón-Saavedra and published in restricted form on 11 May 2022.
The equations posit a transient local discontinuity in the conserved-momentum tensor, sustained by
metabolic substrate drawn from the asset's central nervous system. The discontinuity is bounded in
mass, duration, range, and recurrence, all of which have been experimentally calibrated.
When the effect is active, the suspended object:
has effective inertial mass equal to approximately 0.4% of its rest mass relative to the local Earth
frame
retains its gravitational mass (small objects still fall in earth gravity, but with no resistance to lateral
force)
does not interact mechanically with the asset herself (collision exemption)
resumes its prior linear and angular momentum exactly upon termination (see Tail Momentum,
§2.6)
2.2 Targeting Procedure
Activation requires a focused line-of-sight acquisition of the target, sustained for 1.2 seconds baseline.
This focus is mediated by visual attention; auditory or proprioceptive targeting is not possible. The asset
describes the targeting period internally as "tuning the weight out."
Targeting failure rates by environmental condition:
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
4 / 50
Condition
Targeting failure rate
Dry, clear, daylight
0.4%
Light precipitation
2.1%

7.0%
Fog ≤ 12 m visibility

Targeting is interrupted irreversibly by acoustic shock ≥ 135 dB delivered within the 1.2 s window; the
asset must restart acquisition from zero and incurs a 4.2-second neural recovery delay before re-
attempting.
2.3 Mass Ceiling
The controlled mass ceiling under standard operational conditions is 240 kg. This figure was established
through CBMP testing across five quarterly cycles between 2019 and 2022 and has remained stable
through 2026.
Recorded peak (uncontrolled): 380 kg, observed during the 2018 trigger event when she suspended a
fragmenting block of slate during her fall. The trigger event remains the sole recorded instance above
250 kg and is not reproducible under controlled conditions.
Above the 240 kg ceiling, attempted activation produces one of three outcomes:
1. Activation failure with neural feedback (mild migraine onset) — 41% of cases
2. Partial suspension of a fraction of the target (e.g., one face of a larger object) — 36% of cases
3. Full activation followed by Echo Lock failure mode (see §2.13) — 23% of cases
2.4 Duration
Baseline maximum sustained duration of a single suspension: 0.6 seconds. This figure is hard-capped
under normal physiological conditions and is independent of target mass within the 240 kg ceiling.
Duration extension is achievable via pharmacological augmentation. Under therapeutic dose of Calexin-7
(240 mg, twice daily, see §4.9), tested duration extends to 1.1 seconds. The Cooperative does not
authorize Calexin-7 doses beyond therapeutic levels; off-label dosing of 360 mg in 2022 yielded 1.4 s
duration but produced severe vasoconstrictive side-effects and is prohibited under Cooperative Medical
Standing Order 11.
2.5 Range
Effective operational range: 22 meters measured from the asset's nasion to the target's center of mass.
Theoretical maximum: 41 meters, with substantially degraded reliability; targeting resolution declines
past 28 meters at a rate of approximately 1.4% failure increase per additional meter.
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
5 / 50
Range is not affected by intervening transparent obstacles (glass, ice, polymer film); it is interrupted by
any opaque obstacle within the visual cone regardless of material thickness. The effect propagates
instantaneously at all measured ranges (sub-microsecond resolution).
2.6 Tail Momentum
When the suspension ends, the target resumes its prior linear and angular momentum vector exactly —
direction, magnitude, and rotation. This property is termed Tail Momentum and underpins the Tail-
Strike combat technique (see §5.4).
Tail Momentum has been measured to within ±0.03% accuracy in CBMP impulse-recovery trials. The only
documented deviation occurred during the Antofagasta incident (May 2021), when an asymmetric
thermal gradient applied to a suspended target produced a 2.8% lateral drift; this anomaly has not been
replicated under controlled conditions.
2.7 Cooldown Matrix
The recurrence-rate limits on Inertial Suspension are governed by three interleaved cooldowns:
Cooldown type
Duration
Trigger
Per-target cooldown
11.0 s

this window

Underlies the "Three-Two-One Rule" (§5.2)
Violations of the per-target cooldown produce null activation (no effect, full metabolic cost). Violations
of the global cooldown produce half-duration suspension (0.3 s baseline) and elevated migraine onset
risk.
2.8 Contact Exclusion
The asset cannot suspend an object that she is in direct physical contact with at the moment of trigger.
The contact exclusion applies to:
Held objects (e.g., she cannot suspend a baton she is currently gripping)
Worn equipment
Surfaces she is standing on or pressed against
Other personnel she is touching
She can, however, suspend an object that she releases within ~0.3 seconds prior to trigger. The
standard tactical drill known as "Drop-Tune" exploits this: she releases a throwing baton, lets it travel
briefly, then suspends it mid-flight for repositioning (see §5.5).
2.9 Self-Suspension
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
6 / 50
The asset can target her own body for Inertial Suspension, provided the contact-exclusion rule is
satisfied (i.e., she is not currently in contact with another large mass at the trigger instant). Mid-air self-
suspension is a documented life-saving application — the 2018 trigger event was itself a mid-fall self-
suspension that arrested a 47-meter descent for the 0.6-second window required to reorient.
Self-suspension during ground contact requires brief in-air transitions and is rarely tactically applicable.
Self-suspension while gripping a weapon is forbidden under standing orders due to the contact rule (see
§5, Directive 7).
2.10 Secondary Ability: Drift Read
Drift Read is a passive, involuntary precognitive sense awakened during the Antofagasta incident, 17 May
2021. It manifests as a tactile pre-perception of incoming projectile vectors within approximately 3
meters of the asset's body, perceived as a "draft" or directional pressure sense on the skin.
Drift Read parameters:
Parameter
Value
Range
3.0 m, hard-capped
Lead-time
~0.30 s before projectile arrival
Applicable projectiles
Any solid object travelling ≥ 8 m/s
Inapplicable to
Liquids, plasma, electromagnetic radiation, sound pressure

Yes, degraded by ~60%
Voluntary disengagement
Not possible
Drift Read does not directly enable evasion; it provides directional information that the asset processes
consciously and exploits via reflex or by triggering a fast Inertial Suspension on the incoming object.
2.11 Tertiary Ability: Hold
Hold is a low-mass, extended-duration variant of Inertial Suspension available for objects up to 2 kg. It
permits sustained suspension for up to 4.0 seconds at a metabolic cost approximately equal to a single
full-duration standard suspension.
Hold is restricted to objects she has already successfully suspended once within the past 90 seconds;
Hold is a "re-tune" of an existing inertial signature, not a primary engagement. It cannot be applied to
living tissue and cannot be applied to self.
Operational use: keeping a thrown baton mid-air to choreograph a multi-baton kinetic field (the
Suspended Field technique, §5.6).
2.12 Prohibited Targets
The following are categorically excluded from Inertial Suspension under all known conditions:

2.13 Echo Lock Failure Mode
Echo Lock is a neurological seizure-equivalent observed twice in operational record:
19 November 2020 (training environment, Lima)
14 August 2023 (combat environment, Salta)
Symptoms: a 4-second targeting freeze in which the asset cannot acquire new targets, cannot voluntarily
release a current suspension, and experiences pronounced cervical spasm. Recovery is autonomous; no
permanent neurological damage has been documented. Triggering conditions include:
Attempted suspension above the 240 kg ceiling
Acoustic shock during the targeting window (≥ 145 dB)
Severe hypoglycemia (blood glucose < 58 mg/dL) during sustained operation
Echo Lock occurrence remains rare (2 events / approximately 3,200 logged activations through 31
December 2025).
2.14 Metabolic Cost
Operational metabolic demand at peak operational rate (3 activations/min sustained):
Caloric burn: ~2,100 kcal per operational hour
Sodium loss: 1.4 g/hr
Glycogen depletion: complete within 38 minutes
Required carbohydrate intake during sustained operation: 80 g/hr complex, plus emergency 25 g
fast-acting
Core-temperature elevation: +0.6 °C per operational hour at peak rate
Field nutrition protocol mandates hypertonic glucose gel every 30 minutes during active engagement and
a standardized electrolyte solution (PAMC-issued formulation Bofedal-3, 740 mL/hr) between
activations.
2.15 Neurological Tax
Chronic neurological consequences observed:
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md

8 / 50
Cervical migraines, frequency 3–5 per week without prophylaxis
Pharmacological management: Calexin-7 240 mg twice daily (see §4.9)
Sleep latency increase of ~14 minutes baseline
REM-cycle disruption on operational nights (~22% reduction)
Cumulative biological aging correlate: telomere shortening +2.4 years per operational year
(most recent measurement Q4 2025)
No documented cognitive decline; cognitive performance assessment Q4 2025 within prior
baseline
2.16 Documented Vulnerabilities
In addition to the Echo Lock trigger conditions, the following operational vulnerabilities are formally
documented:
Acoustic shock ≥ 135 dB during targeting interrupts acquisition (§2.2)
Sustained sensory deprivation (visual occlusion > 40 minutes) elicits transient power
suppression
Hypothermia core < 34.6 °C disables Inertial Suspension entirely
Hyperventilation (rapid > 34 breaths/min for > 90 s) degrades targeting by 18%
EM-pulse exposure above 0.4 T does not directly disable the power but causes secondary nerve
dysfunction
Concurrent metahuman countermeasures documented twice (see §7.3 — Tropopausa; §7.10 —
Mar Inverso)

SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md

Within the PAMC roster, SLATEFALL occupies a narrow operational niche. Of 217 currently registered
Cooperative operatives:
38 are Class A (Kinetic), of whom 12 are Class A-2 (Targeted)
Only 4 Class A-2 operatives possess an inertial- or momentum-related primary power

transfer rather than mass)
Comparable adversary capability is suspected in only one open file: Mar Inverso (§7.10), projected
Tier 5
2.19 Research and Re-Evaluation Schedule
Annual re-evaluation of power parameters is mandated under PAMC Scientific Standing Order 4. The next
scheduled CBMP full assessment is 11–14 June 2026 in Lima. Interim quarterly checks are performed by
Dr. Solène Bauer-Cortez at Cuartel Valparaíso.
2.20 Documented Anomalies and Unresolved Mechanics
Four anomalies remain formally classified as Unresolved Mechanics by CBMP and are flagged for future
research cycles:
1. The Antofagasta Drift (May 2021): the singular 2.8% lateral deviation in Tail Momentum recorded
during the Drift Read awakening event. Hypothesis: asymmetric thermal gradient from a nearby
refinery flare. Replication attempts in CBMP labs (4 attempts, 2021–2024) all failed to reproduce
the effect.
2. The "Twin-Lock" hypothesis: during the 14 August 2023 Salta engagement, telemetry recorded a
0.21-second period in which two simultaneously suspended objects exhibited interlocked
termination — both released within 14 milliseconds of each other despite separate activation
timestamps. No mechanism has been proposed.
3. The 2024 Coquimbo dampening: during the 4th Tropopausa engagement (Coquimbo, May
2024), the asset's targeting failure rate dropped to 0.0% across 7 activations — well below the
0.4% baseline. Hypothesis: synergistic interaction with Tropopausa's pressure field. Unverified.
4. REM-cycle bleed: during REM sleep, the asset's Reset Bracers occasionally register low-
amplitude targeting-precursor signals despite no conscious activation. Frequency: ~3 per night. No
external effects have been observed; the phenomenon may be a subconscious artifact rather than
a true power expression.
These anomalies are reviewed each annual cycle and are not currently considered operational risks.

2.22 Power-Onset Pre-Activation Signatures
CBMP has documented three measurable pre-activation physiological signatures detectable
approximately 0.4 seconds before the asset initiates targeting:
Pupillary dilation increase of 11–14% (target-side pupil only)
Skin conductance spike of 2.6 µS on left thenar eminence
Spinal-cord vibrational micro-signature, 78 Hz, recorded via Reset Bracer accelerometers
These signatures are not detectable by external observers without specialized instrumentation, but the
Cooperative classifies them as Operational Tell-Tale Indicators (OTTI) and rotates the asset's pre-
engagement breathing protocol quarterly to mitigate predictive exploitation by adversaries.

Section 3. Origin and Key Historical Events
3.1 Early Life (1987–2005)
Inez Yolanda Calvache-Renström was born on 8 September 1987 at the Hospital Naval de Punta
Arenas, in conditions noted as "uneventful" on her birth record. Her early childhood, until age 12, was
spent in the Magallanes Region, where her father served as a regional army geologist and her mother
operated a small pediatric practice. The family resided at Calle Errázuriz 1422, Punta Arenas — a
residence later sold in 2001.
In late 1999, following her father's reassignment to the Centro de Estudios Geográficos in Santiago, the
family relocated to the capital. Calvache-Renström completed secondary education at the Liceo Manuel
de Salas between 2001 and 2004, graduating with academic honors in geography, mathematics, and
physical education. She represented the school in two consecutive national alpine-skiing championships
(2003 and 2004), placing 7th and 4th respectively in the U18 women's giant slalom.
A 2003 field trip to Glaciar Exploradores in the Aysén Region is recorded in subsequent
autobiographical interviews as a formative experience.
3.2 Military Cadet and Junior Officer Years (2005–2014)
She entered the Escuela Militar Bernardo O'Higgins on 4 March 2005. Cadet rank progression and
notable cycle assignments are summarized in §1.3 and §1.4. She graduated 12 December 2009 with class
rank 14 of 162, receiving the Espada de Honor de Geografía Militar for top scores in topographic
surveying.

On 19 March 2014 she was selected for instructor secondment at CEM Río Blanco, a posting traditionally
indicating fast-track promotion intent.
3.3 Senior Instructor Period (2014–2018)
From 2014 through mid-2018, Calvache-Renström served as a senior instructor at the Centro de
Entrenamiento de Montaña, attaining the rank of Capitán on 1 March 2017. During this period she led 11
instructor cycles, certified 142 mountain warfare graduates, and authored two internal Army doctrine
annexes:
Annex M-14: Rope-Rescue Procedures Above 4,500 m
Annex M-16: Ice-Anchor Selection in Mixed-Substrate Faces
She received the Cruz al Mérito Militar (Tercera Clase) on 7 September 2017 for the successful
extraction of a stranded civilian climbing party from Volcán Tupungato during inclement weather.
3.4 The Cerro Castillo Incident (8 October 2018)
The asset's powers-emergence event occurred on 8 October 2018 at approximately 14:42 local time on
the eastern face of Cerro Castillo (47°15′S, 72°06′W), elevation ~2,318 m at incident point. She was
conducting a solo reconnaissance ascent in preparation for a CEM instructor cycle, climbing a Grade IV-5
mixed slate-and-ice route.
Sequence of events as reconstructed from her helmet recorder, recovered ice tools, and post-incident
geological survey:
1. 14:40:11 — Anchor screw #4 was placed at 2,318 m elevation.
2. 14:41:37 — Rope-end ice tool placement failed in unconsolidated slate.
3. 14:41:52 — A 1.2 m × 0.8 m slate block detached from the face, striking her left brow ridge.
4. 14:41:54 — Anchor screw #4 sheared; she entered free-fall.
5. 14:41:58 — During the fall, she experienced an involuntary first activation of Inertial Suspension,
arresting the descent of a fragmented 380 kg slate mass adjacent to her body for an estimated 0.6
s, allowing reorientation.
6. 14:42:00 — Self-suspension event arrested her own 47-meter fall for the same 0.6-second
window; she resumed motion at reduced trajectory and impacted a snowfield rather than rock.
7. 14:42:14 — Loss of consciousness, traumatic.
She was located by a CEM rescue patrol 4 hours 21 minutes later, evacuated by helicopter to Hospital
Regional de Coyhaique, and transferred 11 October 2018 to Hospital Militar Santiago. Injuries: closed-
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
12 / 50
head trauma, right thumb fracture with nerve damage, mild hypothermia, multiple contusions. No
fatalities; no other personnel were on the mountain at the time.
3.5 Recovery and Powers Confirmation (October 2018 – February 2019)
During her hospitalization, anomalous events were observed by attending nursing staff between 24
October and 11 November 2018: small objects in the immediate vicinity of her bed exhibited transient
"weightless" motion correlating with REM cycles. On 14 November, a controlled observation confirmed
reproducible inertial suspension of a 200-gram steel object at her request.
She was transferred to the Lima Metahuman Assessment Facility (LMAF) on 28 January 2019. PAMC
initial assessment classified her capability as a novel Class A-2 phenomenon. Medical discharge from the
Chilean Army was finalized 28 February 2019.
3.6 PAMC Recruitment and Probationary Period (March 2019 – November 2020)
She signed the Cooperative Service Agreement on 12 March 2019. Codename SLATEFALL assigned 7
May 2019. Probationary deployment cycle:
19 March 2019 – 22 August 2019: residential training, Lima HQ
23 August 2019 – 14 January 2020: paired-cell rotations under senior operative MIRADOR (Cpt.
(ret.) Estefania Pizarroso-Aldama)
15 January 2020 – 19 November 2020: independent probationary deployments, Atacama Sector
The 19 November 2020 training incident at Lima HQ produced the first Echo Lock event (see §2.13).
Investigation concluded the cause was an unauthorized peer-test attempt to suspend a 320 kg test sled.
Subsequent training discipline tightened; the mass ceiling protocol was formalized.
3.7 The Antofagasta Incident and Drift Read Awakening (17 May 2021)
On 17 May 2021 during a containment operation in the port district of Antofagasta (PAMC Case 2020-
0048, see §9.2), the asset suffered a small-arms ambush from elevated cover. During the engagement,
she experienced an involuntary precognitive perception of incoming projectile vectors — the first
manifestation of what would be designated Drift Read (§2.10). The ability has remained continuously
active since.
The Antofagasta operation produced a Successful classification but resulted in a 14-day medical leave for
the asset and the documented onset of post-traumatic symptoms (§1.9).
3.8 Tropopausa First Engagement and the Mendoza Pyrrhic (June 2022)
On 3–5 June 2022, the asset engaged the metahuman adversary Tropopausa / Eliseo Mardones for
the first time, in the Mendoza Free Trade Zone (Case 2022-0123, §9.4). The engagement is classified
Pyrrhic: Tropopausa was contained, but PAMC suffered 3 civilian and 1 operative casualty (Cpl. Adolfo
Yáñez-Ríos, civilian medic, Cell Cóndor).
The Mendoza engagement produced revisions to PAMC engagement protocols against atmospheric-
pressure manipulators and prompted the formal articulation of the Doctrine of Sequential Suspension
(§5.1) on 19 June 2022.
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
13 / 50
3.9 Cell Halcón Formation and Marriage (2022–2023)
Following Mendoza, the asset was promoted to Field Officer, Grade 2 on 1 August 2022, with command
authority over a newly-formed 4-operative cell designated Cell Halcón. Cell composition was finalized 11
October 2022 (see §6.2).
She married Dr. Catalina Renström-Verdugo on 4 November 2023 in a private civil ceremony in Viña del
Mar. Cooperative liaison clearance for the spouse was processed in January 2024.
3.10 Salta Echo Lock and Quebrantadero (August 2023)
On 14 August 2023, during Case 2024-0202 in Salta Province, Argentina (see §9.7), the asset
experienced her second documented Echo Lock event while attempting suspension of a 280 kg vehicular
component. The Salta engagement against Quebrantadero / Wilma Sandoval-Iqui produced significant
civilian casualties (11 confirmed) and is classified Catastrophic. A formal Cooperative inquiry concluded
in March 2024 with no censure of the asset but with three recommended protocol changes, all
subsequently adopted.
3.11 Lead Officer Status and 2024–2025 Operations
On 9 February 2024, the asset was promoted to Lead Officer (Field, Grade 3) with expanded
command authority. During 2024 and 2025 she led 23 logged operations, including:
Tarapacá Concession compound raid (Case 2024-0034)
Tallinn-Asunción intelligence rotation (joint with European liaison)
Lima Audiencia counter-audit (Case 2025-0019)
Potosí Cabrestante neutralization (Case 2025-0156)
Cumulative operational hours under power during 2024–2025: approximately 612.
3.12 Current Deployment (2026)
As of revision date (11 April 2026), the asset is engaged in Case 2026-0008, classification restricted,
with operational territory spanning the Bolivian altiplano and northern Chilean copper belt. The case is
open and ongoing; specifics are withheld from this revision per PAMC Compartmentalization Order 3-

5.content:
Section 5. Operational Tactics and Combat Doctrine
5.1 The Doctrine of Sequential Suspension
The asset's formal combat doctrine, the Doctrine of Sequential Suspension (DSS), was articulated by
the asset on 19 June 2022 in the aftermath of the Mendoza Pyrrhic engagement (§3.8). It is now PAMC-
recognized doctrine for Class A-2 (Targeted) operatives and forms part of the standard curriculum at the
Lima Operational Academy.
The doctrine rests on six core principles:
1. Suspension is a decisive resource, not a continuous capability. Treat each activation as
ammunition.
2. Sequence determines lethality. A single suspension rarely wins an engagement; ordered chains
of three define the doctrine.
3. Acquisition is the vulnerability. Protect the 1.2-second targeting window above all.
4. Tail momentum is the payoff. Plan terminal vectors before activating.
5. Cooldown discipline is non-negotiable. Operate as if the matrix is enforced by a third party.
6. Withdraw before exhaustion. Three activations and reset.
5.2 The Three-Two-One Rule
The asset's signature tactical heuristic, drilled into Cell Halcón operations and taught at Lima Academy:
Three activations maximum per 60-second window
Two active sightlines maintained at any moment
One pre-anchored extraction route held in reserve
Violations of any element trigger an automatic call-off by the cell handler (Lt. Bauer-Cortez monitors via
Reset Bracer telemetry). Documented violations: 7 since 2022, of which 5 produced operational
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
22 / 50
anomalies.
5.3 Tier-Based Engagement Protocols
PAMC Threat Tier definitions are detailed in §7.1. Engagement protocols differ as follows:
Threat
tier
Asset's permitted engagement

Tier 1

Tier 2

The asset is not authorized to engage Tier 5 threats solo under any condition.
5.4 The Tail-Strike Technique
The Tail-Strike exploits Tail Momentum (§2.6) to deliver compounded impacts that cannot be achieved
by linear projectile motion alone:
1. The asset throws a baton at a target.
2. Mid-flight, she suspends the baton, halting it in space.
3. While suspended, she physically repositions the baton (e.g., rotating it, advancing it along its path
with a hand-push).
4. On release, the baton resumes its original momentum from its new position — striking with vector
consistent with original throw but originating closer.
The result: a strike delivered at full thrown velocity but from an arbitrary, target-adjacent location.
Adversary counter-prediction of trajectory becomes nearly impossible. Cumulative Tail-Strike
applications logged through 2025: 312 (§2.17).
5.5 The Drop-Tune Manoeuvre
Drop-Tune is the standard activation pattern around the contact-exclusion rule (§2.8):
1. Asset releases an object she is holding.
2. After ~0.3 seconds, the contact-exclusion field clears.
3. Asset suspends the released object before it strikes anything.
The technique permits a meaningful one-second window between release and engagement target
acquisition. The exact 0.3 s minimum has been internalized as muscle memory; the asset's Cell Halcón
training videos use a metronome cue to instill the rhythm in new operatives.
5.6 The Suspended Field Technique
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
23 / 50
The Suspended Field uses the tertiary Hold ability (§2.11) to maintain multiple low-mass objects
(typically batons) in mid-air simultaneously, creating a transient kinetic-cover zone:
Maximum simultaneously held objects: 4 (matching available activations within a 60-s window
minus a 1-suspension reserve)
Hold duration per object: up to 4 s
Field deployment time: ~12 seconds
Field collapse: simultaneous release of all held objects with full Tail Momentum, producing a
directed multi-baton strike
Cumulative Suspended Field deployments through 2025: 89.
5.7 Mountain-Warfare Carryover Doctrine
The asset's Chilean Army background informs four standing carryover doctrines:
1. Snow-Anchor Triage: never advance past an anchor without verifying it. Carried over from rope-
rescue protocols.
2. Three-Point Contact: always maintain three secure contacts (hand, foot, anchor) when traversing
vertical terrain — applied to mark-placement scenarios.
3. Altitude Acclimatization Protocol: above 3,800 m, mandatory 48-hour acclimatization period
unless emergency engagement.
4. Whiteout Withdrawal: below 12 m visibility, withdrawal is automatic regardless of mission priority.
5.8 Civilian Collateral Threshold
PAMC Standing Order 9 specifies a zero-tolerance threshold below 80% confidence that no civilian
harm will occur from any planned activation. The asset is empowered to abort engagement at her sole
discretion if civilian risk exceeds the threshold. Documented aborts on civilian-risk grounds since 2019:
31.
5.9 Reconnaissance Sequencing
Standard pre-engagement reconnaissance for cell-led operations follows the 72-hour saturation
protocol:
T−72h to T−48h: passive surveillance (Pajarera asset network if available)
T−48h to T−24h: physical site survey by Sgt. Quispe-Linares
T−24h to T−12h: electromagnetic survey by Lt. Bauer-Cortez
T−12h to T−4h: medical and metabolic prep (Cpl. Vendrame)
T−4h to T−0h: final cell briefing and equipment check
Solo deployments allow compression of this schedule to a 24-hour minimum, but not less.
5.10 The Four-Minute Window
The Four-Minute Window is the asset's standing extraction discipline: from initiation of withdrawal, the
cell must reach the pre-anchored extraction route within four minutes. The figure derives from observed
adversary response times during the Mendoza engagement and has held as a planning constant since
2022.
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
24 / 50
5.11 The Twelve Combat Directives
The asset's published standing combat directives (PAMC Internal Bulletin 22-19):
1. Acquire targets only when extraction is mapped.
2. Never activate without verifying the contact-exclusion radius.
3. Hold one activation in reserve until extraction.
4. Cooldown discipline overrides tactical opportunity.
5. Civilian risk overrides cooldown discipline.
6. Engage Tier 4 only with two extraction routes verified.
7. Never self-suspend while gripping a weapon.
8. Never engage above 4,800 m without acclimatization.
9. Never engage below 12 m visibility.
10. Withdraw at 80% targeting fatigue (Reset Bracer abort threshold).
11. Always release a captured Calexin-7 supply to the medic before engagement.
12. Never break radio silence on the Yatiri-3 burst comms during targeting.

7.3 Tropopausa / Eliseo Mardones-Vodanovic
Codename: Tropopausa Civilian identity: Eliseo Mardones-Vodanovic, born 1981, San Bernardo, Chile
Profession (cover): atmospheric scientist; formerly affiliated with the Centro de Estudios Climáticos
de Valparaíso (CECV) Tier: 4
Powers: atmospheric pressure manipulation within a 40-meter radius. Capable of inducing local pressure
differentials up to ±0.62 atm in a 4-second interval, producing acute barotrauma in unprotected
personnel and disabling small aircraft engines. He cannot create wind ex nihilo but can redistribute
existing pressure gradients with high precision.
Documented limits:
Field-creation cooldown: 18 seconds between activations
Maximum sustained field duration: 22 seconds before metabolic collapse
Vulnerable to acoustic disruption above 140 dB
Cannot operate above 5,800 m altitude (atmospheric thinning disables binding)
Class A-2 (Targeted) — same scientific class as SLATEFALL, but on a different operational axis
Threat profile vs. SLATEFALL: Tropopausa's pressure fields can interfere with SLATEFALL's targeting
acquisition by introducing tinnitus-grade interior-ear disturbance. Acoustic shock from his pressure
pulses has produced one documented targeting interruption (Mendoza, 2022).
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
31 / 50
Engagement history: 4 documented engagements through 2025 (Mendoza 2022, Antofagasta 2023,
Iquique 2023, Coquimbo 2024). Currently contained at the PAMC Restraint Facility, Iquique (since
November 2024).
7.4 El Sereno
Codename: El Sereno Civilian identity: Unknown; suspected male, Andean ancestry, age 30–45 Tier: 3
Powers: nocturnal sensory enhancement (multi-spectral, including thermal IR to 14 μm) and a
documented capacity for kinetic dampening of his own footfalls and respiration. Approaches targets in
absolute silence; documented sound floor during approach: −12 dB SPL (below ambient).
Documented limits:
All capabilities suppressed by direct sunlight or visible-spectrum illumination above 1,200 lux
Documented hearing impairment in left ear (left-side targeting penalty)
Operates exclusively between local dusk and 1.5 hours before local dawn
Threat profile vs. SLATEFALL: El Sereno is the asset's most dangerous nocturnal threat because his
stealth approaches degrade SLATEFALL's targeting acquisition (which depends on visual perception).
Drift Read provides partial counter-defense within 3 m.
Engagement history: 3 confirmed engagements (Iquique 2023, Antofagasta 2024, Arica 2025); all
inconclusive. He is the longest-standing at-large adversary on the asset's roster.
7.5 Quebrantadero / Wilma Sandoval-Iqui
Codename: Quebrantadero Civilian identity: Wilma Sandoval-Iqui, born 1979, Oruro, Bolivia Profession
(cover): orthopedic surgeon (revoked license 2022) Tier: 4
Powers: induces controlled bone-density fluctuation in mammalian subjects within a 14-meter radius by
line-of-sight contact. Effects range from temporary fragility (osteopenia-equivalent for ~6 hours) to
acute fracture cascades.
Documented limits:
Cannot affect non-mammalian biology
Targeting requires 2.4-second focus, with audible vocalization
Counterable by lead-lined shielding (≥ 6 mm thickness)
Effect non-cumulative; subsequent re-targeting within 30 minutes produces only 12% additional
density change
Threat profile vs. SLATEFALL: Quebrantadero's effect is targeted at biological tissue, not inanimate
matter — meaning SLATEFALL's primary kinetic implements are unaffected, but the asset herself is highly
vulnerable. The 14 August 2023 Salta engagement (§3.10) produced 11 civilian casualties from a
Quebrantadero pulse the asset failed to interdict.
Engagement history: 2 documented engagements (Salta 2023, Cuzco 2024). Currently at-large.
7.6 Underwriter / Magnus Sletten

Codename: Underwriter (asset-side designation; he holds no internal codename) Civilian identity:
Magnus Sletten, age 58, Norwegian-Argentine dual citizen Position: PAMC Inspector General
(Administrative Compliance), Lima HQ Tier: 2 (bureaucratic threat assessment; no kinetic capability)
Description: Inspector General Sletten is classified an adversary not because of metahuman capability
but because of sustained adversarial bureaucratic action against the asset's operational profile. Between
2024 and 2026 he initiated eleven formal audits of SLATEFALL operations, three of which sought
disciplinary referral; all three were dismissed. His professional position renders him institutionally
protected; engagement protocols are therefore non-kinetic and procedural only.
Threat profile vs. SLATEFALL: the threat is operational restriction, not physical harm. Case 2025-0019
(§9.8) was a counter-audit defensive engagement, terminating in audit reversal by the Director-General.
7.7 Cabrestante / Pablo Eyzaguirre-Pinto
Codename: Cabrestante Civilian identity: Pablo Eyzaguirre-Pinto, born 1975, Calama, Chile Profession
(cover): mining-machinery engineer Tier: 3
Powers: none innate; classified as a metahuman-adjacent adversary by virtue of his cybernetic
augmentation. His augments include:
Reinforced cervical and thoracic skeleton (titanium-composite, +18 kg total mass)
Hydraulic right arm rated to 4.2-ton lift, 1.4-ton sustained
Optical implant providing 8× zoom and thermal vision
Subcutaneous power-cell array, 14-hour duty cycle
Documented limits:
Augments fail catastrophically below −12 °C ambient
EMP exposure above 0.2 T disables optical and hydraulic systems for 4–12 hours
Augment maintenance requires monthly servicing at a specialized facility (one suspected facility

Threat profile vs. SLATEFALL: Cabrestante's hydraulic arm exceeds the 240 kg mass ceiling for direct
Inertial Suspension, but separate suspended-component attacks (e.g., suspending his arm at the elbow
joint) have proven effective. Combat record vs. SLATEFALL: 1 engagement (Potosí, September 2025,
Case 2025-0156, §9.9), captured.

7.10 Mar Inverso (Projected Tier 5)
Codename: Mar Inverso Civilian identity: Unknown Tier: projected 5; unconfirmed First indication: 22
December 2025, intelligence intercept (source: Pajarera)
Mar Inverso is the asset's most pressing emergent threat. Intelligence intercepted in late 2025 references
a metahuman with inertial inversion capability — projected to be the operational inverse of
SLATEFALL's power, capable of multiplying the inertia of target objects rather than nullifying it. No direct
engagement has occurred; physical evidence is limited to two unexplained vehicle incidents (Mendoza
port, August 2025; La Paz suburb, November 2025) in which heavy vehicles exhibited momentary mass-
spikes inconsistent with conservation principles.
If confirmed, Mar Inverso would represent the only known operational counter-symmetric threat to
SLATEFALL's primary power. PAMC has elevated the file to Andean Operations Bureau Priority 1 as of
January 2026. The asset is not currently authorized to engage Mar Inverso solo.

Designation: The Audiencia Type: bureaucratic-adversary cluster within PAMC oversight bodies
Membership: estimated 7–12 senior administrators (4 confirmed) Tier: 2 (bureaucratic, non-kinetic)
The Audiencia is a faction within PAMC's senior administration that opposes the current operational
profile of Class A-2 assets, including SLATEFALL. The Underwriter (§7.6) is associated but not formally a

low-tier Concession affiliate. First post-action evaluation prescribed Calexin-7.
9.4 Case 2022-0123: Tropopausa First Engagement (Mendoza)
Case number: 2022-0123
Date: 3–5 June 2022
Location: Mendoza Free Trade Zone, Argentina
Classification: Classified (Tier-2)
Outcome: Pyrrhic
PAMC casualties: 1 operative KIA (Cpl. Adolfo Yáñez-Ríos, Cell Cóndor); 3 civilians killed
Adversary casualties: Tropopausa contained but escaped after 8 hours
Notes: Engagement triggered formal articulation of the Doctrine of Sequential Suspension. Asset
suffered ear barotrauma from pressure pulse; recovered without permanent damage. Case led to
revised tactical protocols against pressure manipulators.
9.5 Case 2023-0067: El Sereno Pursuit (Iquique)
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
40 / 50
Case number: 2023-0067
Date: 11–14 April 2023
Location: Iquique, Tarapacá Region, Chile
Classification: Open
Outcome: Inconclusive
PAMC casualties: none
Adversary casualties: none (El Sereno disengaged at dawn)
Notes: First contact with El Sereno. Demonstrated the limitations of nocturnal engagement
profiles. Recommended development of high-lux operation kit; partially implemented 2024.
9.6 Case 2024-0034: Tarapacá Concession Raid
Case number: 2024-0034
Date: 17–18 February 2024
Location: Tarapacá Region, Chile (compound near Pozo Almonte)
Classification: Declassified Q1 2024 (Report PAMC-PR-2024-002)
Outcome: Successful
PAMC casualties: 1 wounded (Sgt. Quispe-Linares, leg laceration, 8-day recovery)
Adversary casualties: 14 Concession affiliates captured; 2 killed in firefight; 1 metahuman
("Cataract") escaped
Notes: Major operational success demonstrating Cell Halcón's full capability. The asset deployed
Suspended Field for the first time in combat. Detained personnel led to seizure of USD $4.2 million
in Concession assets.
9.7 Case 2024-0202: Quebrantadero Showdown (Salta)
Case number: 2024-0202
Date: 14 August 2023
Location: Salta city center, Salta Province, Argentina
Classification: Classified (Tier-3); inquiry concluded March 2024
Outcome: Catastrophic
PAMC casualties: 0 operative; 11 civilian deaths, 27 civilian injuries
Adversary casualties: Quebrantadero escaped
Notes: Second Echo Lock event during the engagement (§3.10). Quebrantadero's pulse struck a
civilian-density urban target before SLATEFALL could interdict. Formal inquiry recommended three
protocol changes (all adopted). Case remains the asset's most prominent operational failure and is
a featured case study at Lima Academy.
9.8 Case 2025-0019: Audiencia Counter-Audit (Lima)
Case number: 2025-0019
Date: 22 January – 18 February 2025
Location: PAMC HQ, Lima
Classification: Sealed (internal proceeding)
Outcome: Successful (audit reversed)
PAMC casualties: none
Adversary casualties: Underwriter referral dismissed; one Audiencia member reassigned
SLATEFALL_DOSSIER.md
2026-05-18
41 / 50
Notes: Procedural defensive action; included formal testimony by SLATEFALL on 4 February 2025.
Director-General Espósito-Tagle issued the final ruling.
9.9 Case 2025-0156: Cabrestante Neutralization (Potosí)
Case number: 2025-0156
Date: 18–22 September 2025
Location: Cerro Rico, Potosí, Bolivia
Classification: Declassified Q4 2025 (Report PAMC-PR-2025-011)
Outcome: Successful
PAMC casualties: 1 minor (Lt. Bauer-Cortez, hand burn)
Adversary casualties: Cabrestante captured; 3 Iron Tributary affiliates captured
Notes: Coordinated cell engagement utilizing segmented suspension (suspending the hydraulic
arm at the elbow joint rather than the full augment). Demonstrated tactical workaround for above-
ceiling targets. Cabrestante is currently in CBMP medical custody for augment study.
9.9.5 Case 2021-0091: Atacama Altiplano (Drift Read Confirmation)
Case number: 2021-0091
Date: 14–22 September 2021
Location: Atacama altiplano, near Socaire, Antofagasta Region, Chile
Classification: Declassified Q2 2022 (partial)
Outcome: Partial
PAMC casualties: none
Adversary casualties: 1 Concession affiliate captured, 2 escaped
Notes: First operationally validated deployment of the newly emerged Drift Read ability. The asset
detected and interdicted three incoming small-arms rounds across a 36-hour engagement window.
Drift Read was formally added to the asset's capability registry on 30 September 2021 following
this case. CBMP collected the bulk of its early Drift Read telemetry during this operation.
9.9.6 Case 2024-0156: Tallinn-Asunción Liaison
Case number: 2024-0156
Date: 4–17 August 2024
Location: Asunción, Paraguay (observer territory) and Tallinn, Estonia (joint EUMR coordination)
Classification: Classified (Tier-2)
Outcome: Successful
PAMC casualties: none
Adversary casualties: 3 captured (mixed Iron Tributary / EUMR adversary network); 1 fatality
(non-PAMC operative)
Notes: Rare extra-territorial deployment authorized by Director-General Espósito-Tagle. Joint
operation with EUMR operatives HOLLOWMARK and VERIDIAN. The asset spent 9 days in Tallinn
coordinating intelligence handoff. First instance of the asset's Inertial Suspension being
demonstrated to non-PAMC liaisons under controlled observation. Produced a 14-page joint after-
action report.

Notes: Triennial audit of all 19 micro-caches (§8.4). 3 caches required content rotation; 1 (Salar de
